excerpts.excerpt:
DILLER
Lis’?
She looks up. It’s not hysteria: she’s just finished cleaning
the broiler pan. The kitchen is spotless.
DILLER (CONT’D)
Aw, baby. If you got all the way to the
oven, you’re breakin’ my heart.
LISA
Some women cry, some clean.
She rises and meets his eyes.
LISA (CONT’D)
So this house is gonna be spotless.
Until you get home and throw your socks
all over the floor.
She hugs him, still clutching the dirty rag.
EXT. BUS - FORT CAMPBELL - 4:35 AM
MILO’s teenage son is crying, despite every effort not to.
Milo pops him lightly in the shoulder.
MILO
Come on now, big guy. I need you. You
got to take care of your mom.
His son nods, mouth quivering. Milo hugs him roughly.

COLONEL MULHOLLAND (V.O.)
Six special forces teams are here.
On a wide open leach field of latrine holes, an Uzbek soldier
is squatting, reading a newspaper.
COLONEL MULHOLLAND (V.O.)
We’re guests of the fine country of
Uzbekistan, who have graciously opened
their doors to us.
INT. BRIEFING TENT - K2 - LATE AFTERNOON
We catch up to Colonel Mulholland addressing the six teams.
NELSON sits in the center, surrounded by his men. He has a
pile of notebooks and material in front of him. All the
soldiers are growing out their beards to fit in with Afghans.
COLONEL MULHOLLAND
19 men attacked us on September 11th.
12 of you will be first to respond.
He scans the eyes around the room: Diller, looking hard;
Black, nervous; Nelson, resolved.
COLONEL MULHOLLAND (CONT’D)
Only one team will be chosen to go in
country. First time Special Forces has
ever been the lead element. You’re
going to be soldiers and diplomats.

INT. NELSON'S TEAM TENT - K2 - DUSK
As Nelson finishes a LAST LETTER to Jean...
LIAISON OFFICER (O.S.)
You guys know the drill...
The liaison officer passes man to man with a plastic bag.
LIAISON OFFICER (CONT’D)
Any personal artifacts. In the event
you’re captured, any item that could be
used against you for torture...
Spencer puts in a picture of Marcha and Jake. Falls drops in
a picture of his parents, including his ATTRACTIVE MOTHER.
COFFERS
Can I have the picture of Falls’ Mom?
We end of NELSON, sitting beside Diller. He shows him a
picture of his daughter, Maddy. As the liaison officer
approaches, Nelson slips the PHOTO into his boot. Then he
meets eyes with Diller.
DILLER
Right. Nobody’s getting captured. We
carry out the mission...
NELSON
Or we die with our boots on.
Diller smiles solemnly, a look of brewing respect on his
face. He slips a picture of LISA into his boot as well.
CLOSE ON THEIR UNIFORMS as NAME TAGS are cut off...

Spencer nods, setting back into the darkness. Nelson keeps
his eyes out the doorway.
ON THE HORIZON
Light slowly begins to come through, like a photograph
developing. Silhouettes form, the horizon clarifies. A
rooster crows. As the sun colors a dusty landscape, there
comes a distant sound: Gunfire. Pop-pop-pop.
INT. THE ALAMO - ROOM - DAWN - CONTINUOUS
Nelson jerks awake at the sound. He grabs his rifle, looking
out at empty mountains in pale light.
Pop - pop - pop. He hears more shots from down the valley,
where he spies a PLUME OF DUST. Crackling like firecrackers,
there’s more small-arms fire. The rest of the team is waking,
grabbing rifles, chambering bullets - the rattles and clicks
of a unit preparing to fight out of a dead sleep.
MICHAELS
We’re taking fire.
At the doorway, Nelson squints at the moving dust. Bennett
and Ramirez are locked and loaded.
NELSON
Coffers, check the courtyard for an
ambush.

Bravo Cell (Spencer, Milo, Essex, Black, Falls, and Jones)
all gather around the Alamo, amused by the sight.
NELSON (CONT’D)
You get raked over this rough ground,
you’ll die.
Bennett’s horse starts to saunter away.
NELSON (CONT’D)
Show the horse you’re in charge. If
you’re not sure, neither’s he.
Diller is a huge man, and on his bowlegged horse he looks as
if he’s trying to settle on a Shetland pony.
Nearby, Ramirez has the shortest yaboo of all.
COFFERS
Figures. Ramirez got a low-rider.
Ramirez just slaps Coffers’ horse on the butt -- and it takes
off running a few yards. In a cloud of dust, Coffers shouts
and pulls back on the reins until the horse throws him.
The Afghans look on in disbelief: these are our allies!?
Nelson looks off into the distance, where Dostum and his
horsemen are already cresting the hill.
NELSON
He’s not waiting.

Dostum grabs Nelson by the shoulder, faux-fatherly for a
moment - then more like he’s showing off his strength.
DOSTUM
“From above,” you say. This is where
you will see that we have the biggest
difference. It seems there are many men
above you. While for me...
(glancing upward)
There is only God.
EXT. HIGH PLATEAU - OVERLOOK - DAWN
On an 8,000 foot ridge filled with Dostum’s troops, Nelson
waits as the attendants arrange a carpet and pillows. There’s
absurdity to their fastidiousness in heavy winds. They motion
that it’s ready and Dostum sits onto his pillow. Nelson joins
him. Looking like they’re on a magic carpet, they peer at
hills gouged by decades of fighting. Behind them, portable
SOLAR CELLS power the radios.
Dostum points at something far in the distance.
DOSTUM
There. Mullah Razzak’s men.
Nelson takes his binoculars from his pack.
THROUGH THE BINOS: Nelson scans rugged terrain.
NELSON
I don’t see anything.
Dostum raises the binoculars up to...

NELSON (CONT’D)
Sir? They have reinforcements!
Dostum faces the battlefield as it changes shape. Nelson
pivots back to Michaels, ashen. Then Nelson begins down the
ridge.
EXT. TALIBAN LINES - OUTSIDE BESCHAM - CONTINUOUS
CLOSE ON THE TALIBAN TANKS. Turrets rotate toward the
approaching cavalry charge.
ON THE LAL’S HORSEMEN -- They leap trenches as - Boom! -
blast rips through them.
FAKIR -- pivots his horse to retreat. But a bullet pierces
his back, spraying blood through an exit wound.
Nelson rushes downhill toward the battle to where Fakir is
retreating toward him. There’s a moment of relief as Nelson
sees him, upright in the saddle, trotting ahead.
NELSON
Jesus, you tough sonofabitch, I
thought...
But as the horse continues ahead, Nelson sees -
- FAKIR is already dead in the saddle, held upright against a
pommel and the reins. He bounces for a few more strides, then
falls off onto the rocks, lifeless.
Nelson can hardly breathe as he looks down at Fakir’s vacant
eyes, staring at the sky. Rockets cut the air overhead.

EXT. ROCKY HILLSIDE - AFTERNOON
From beyond a mountain range, plumes of smoke rise. The
echoing roar of heavy guns sounds like a far-off
thunderstorm. Dostum’s troops are heavily engaged. Nelson
sweats beside Michaels.
NELSON
Michaels?
MICHAELS
All set for round two, sir.
EXT. BESCHAM BATTLEFIELD - DAY
AFGHAN MILITIAMAN
Allahu Akbarrrr!!!
A wave of Dostum’s horsemen crest a hill and charge down the
other side toward the Taliban lines. Across the scorched
earth, the Taliban guns open up. Mortars drop. Rocket-
propelled grenades whiz past.
Beside Nelson and FALLS, Dostum kicks his horse.
DOSTUM
This is a bad place now.
(circling Nelson)
Come! The two best American horsemen!
Follow me!
NELSON
Michaels! Stay on the headset!

NELSON (V.O.)
“The medical care if injured, it’s a
donkey ride to the doctor.”
-- SCOTT BLACK, drenched with blood, tries to save an AFGHAN
SOLDIER from internal bleeding.
NELSON (V.O.)
“The Afghans are remarkable people.
These are men who have nothing, yet
they have offered us everything...”
The patient’s eyes meet Black’s. Suddenly, they glaze over -
as the patient dies in front of him.
NELSON (V.O.)
“...their lives.”
Black gently closes the soldier’s eyes.
INT. PENTAGON PRESS CONFERENCE - WASHINGTON, DC - DAY
Sec. of Defense DONALD RUMSFELD stands at a podium, holding a
printout of Nelson’s e-mail, as a mass of journalists and TV
crews surround him. CAPTION OVER: “OCTOBER 29.”
RUMSFELD
(reading)
“Everywhere I go the Afghans tell me
they are glad the U.S.A. has come. They
speak of their hopes for a better
Afghanistan once the Taliban are gone.
But for now I’m dealing with reality.”

As Dostum grabs the map - there’s a fiery rage in his eyes.
DOSTUM
Tiangi Gap is 2 miles long... between
cliffs. Razzak’s best weapons are
hidden here. Machine guns. Mortars.
Heavy armor. And this is not even the
same Taliban. This will be the 055
Brigade: foreign fighters. Al Qaeda.
They will not give an inch. They prefer
death.
Nelson is staring ahead at Dostum’s blinding anger.
DOSTUM (CONT’D)
The Americans have given Mazar-i-Sharif
to Atta - while they have given me
nothing... while they have given me
you.
Nelson just takes this attack, absorbing it.
DOSTUM (CONT’D)
I will not die for this.
NELSON
Then I request five hundred of your
best cheriks at dawn. My team’s going
through the Gap with or without you.

The SATELLITE DISH wobbles.
He surfs downhill in a landslide. He comes to the very edge
of the cliff, teetering, managing to keep his balance as he
looks straight over the edge at a two-hundred foot drop.
Below in the canyon, he sees: A FLATBED TRUCK hauling a
massive load of 9-foot-long projectiles. Stunned, Diller
seizes the heavy dish and begins running uphill amid gunfire.
DILLER
They got a BM-21, a goddamn monster.
EXT. THE TIANGI GAP - MORNING
In the aftermath of bombing, the canyon is scorched. From
DEEP CAVES in the stone walls, THE 055 BRIGADE begins to
crawl out in black robes - an infestation.
Out of holes deep in the rocks, they begin to drive out a
huge MOBILE MISSILE LAUNCHER: the BM-21.
ON DOSTUM AND HIS MEN
They ride in silence. There’s only the echoing sounds of
hooves on the stone.
HANDHELD SHOT, DIZZYING -- Nelson scans cliffs for snipers.
EXT. EASTERN FLANK - MORNING
Milo, Essex, and Winehouse stand in their trench, flanked by
Afghan soldiers. Najeeb stays near Milo. They’re silent,
staring tensely down at...
